prerequisites: A reachable Rocket.Chat workspace whose base URL you can supply as the endpoint (for example https://your-workspace.rocket.chat).
A Rocket.Chat user account on that workspace that is allowed to create personal access tokens. The Create Personal Access Tokens permission is controlled by the workspace administrator.
A personal access token and its associated user ID, which are shown together only once when the token is generated and must be saved at that time.

authMethods.name: Personal Access Token (token + user_id)
authMethods.summary: Rocket.Chat authenticates REST API calls with two values generated together from Profile > Personal Access Tokens: the token and the user ID. The connector sends them as the X-Auth-Token and X-User-Id request headers. Provide the token in the secret token field and the matching ID in the user_id field.

setupSteps.title: Identify your Rocket.Chat endpoint
setupSteps.body: Determine the base URL of your Rocket.Chat workspace (for example https://your-workspace.rocket.chat). You will enter this as the required endpoint config field so the connector knows which server to query.

setupSteps.title: Generate a personal access token
setupSteps.body: In Rocket.Chat, open your profile and go to Profile > Personal Access Tokens. Enter a token name, choose your two-factor authentication preference, and click Add. Confirm with your password or 2FA code if prompted. Rocket.Chat then displays both your user ID and the access token. Copy both immediately, since they can only be viewed once.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials and configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Set endpoint to your workspace base URL, token to the generated personal access token (the secret field), and user_id to the user ID shown alongside the token. All three fields are required. The connector authenticates by sending the token and user_id as the X-Auth-Token and X-User-Id headers.

sources.title: Manage Personal Access Tokens - Rocket.Chat Documentation
